name: SurrealDB MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official SurrealDB MCP server from SurrealDB enabling AI agents to interact with SurrealDB multi-model database — executing SurrealQL queries, managing tables and records, traversing graph relationships, and integrating SurrealDB's unified document, relational, and graph database capabilities into agent-driven data workflows.
use_cases: Executing SurrealQL queries for document and relational data from data agents
Traversing graph relationships with SurrealQL RELATE from knowledge graph agents
Managing real-time subscriptions and live queries from event-driven agents
Accessing multi-model data in unified queries from integration agents
Building AI application backends with SurrealDB from development agents
Exploring schema and record structure from data discovery agents
not_for: Teams using PostgreSQL, MongoDB, or Neo4j (well-established alternatives)
Production workloads at massive scale (SurrealDB is newer and still maturing)
Teams needing battle-tested database tooling for critical systems
best_when: An agent needs a modern multi-model database that combines document, relational, and graph in one system — especially for AI application backends or greenfield projects.
avoid_when: You need a proven database for critical production data — SurrealDB is newer with less enterprise track record than PostgreSQL or MongoDB.

auth.notes: SurrealDB root, namespace, or database level authentication. Username/password or JWT tokens. Namespace and database must be specified. Record-level access control via SurrealDB permissions. Multi-tenancy via namespaces.

pricing.free_tier_limits: SurrealDB Community: free, open source, self-hosted
pricing.paid_tiers: SurrealDB Cloud: in development; pricing TBD
pricing.notes: SurrealDB is open source (Apache 2.0/BSL). Self-hosting is completely free. Managed cloud offering is in development. Official SurrealDB MCP server from SurrealDB org.

known_agent_gotchas: SurrealQL is unique — not standard SQL; has record links, graph traversals, and live queries
Namespace AND database must be selected for every connection
Table permissions (SELECT, CREATE, UPDATE, DELETE) must be set per table
SurrealDB is pre-1.0 maturity — API may change between versions
Record IDs use thing:id format (e.g., user:john) — different from traditional numeric IDs
Official SurrealDB MCP — newer project; features may lag behind SurrealDB itself
agent_readiness.security_notes: TLS configurable. Username/password or JWT auth with namespace/database scoping. Record-level permissions. Official SurrealDB MCP. Configure TLS for production. Use database-scoped credentials.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS configurable. Username/password with namespace/database scoping. Record-level permissions. Official SurrealDB MCP. Configure TLS. Use namespace-scoped credentials.
